commit.message:
Code review improvements (#5)

* Code review improvements

Bug fixes:
- Fix Modelfile SYSTEM parameter parsing (result was discarded)
- Handle empty tool arguments in tool_parser.py
- Improve model not found error message with config path hint

New endpoints:
- /api/warmup - Preload model into VRAM to reduce first-request latency
- /api/abort - Ollama API compatibility (no-op, client should disconnect)
- /api/unload - Manually unload a model from VRAM
- /metrics - Prometheus-style metrics endpoint

Enhancements:
- Add active_refs field to /api/ps response
- Add CORS middleware for browser-based clients
- Add troubleshooting section and model compatibility matrix to README

All 381 tests pass.

* Fix ruff formatting in manage and status routers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove dead Metrics class and /metrics endpoint

Counters were never incremented anywhere, so the endpoint always
returned zeros. Also had class-level attributes and thread-safety
issues. Can be re-added with proper instrumentation later.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Switch Claude Code Review to native direct_prompt approach

The plugin-based approach (code-review@claude-code-plugins) was
completing successfully but never posting review comments due to
tool permission denials in CI. Use the action's built-in review
capability instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: correct access modifier inconsistencies on internal types (#1029)

## Summary
- Change `public` members on `internal` types to `internal` across
`KnownSymbols`, `MoqKnownSymbolExtensions`, `DiagnosticExtensions`,
`MockDetectionHelpers`, and `NamedTypeSymbolExtensions`
- Change `MockBehaviorDiagnosticAnalyzerBase.AnalyzeCore` from `internal
abstract` to `private protected abstract`, and update both subclass
overrides to match
- Remove dead code (`TryReportMockBehaviorDiagnostic`,
`TryHandleMissingMockBehaviorParameter`) from
`MockBehaviorDiagnosticAnalyzerBase` that was never called by any
subclass

## Test plan
- [x] Solution builds with zero warnings and zero errors
- [x] All 2901 existing tests pass
- [x] No behavioral changes; access modifiers only restrict visibility,
never widen it
- [x] Verified no `InternalsVisibleTo` dependencies exist (Common is a
shared-items project compiled directly into consuming assemblies)
- [x] Confirmed `AnalyzeCore` overrides in
`SetExplicitMockBehaviorAnalyzer` and `SetStrictMockBehaviorAnalyzer`
compile with `private protected override`

Closes #987

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Redesign light/deep model: light=pointers, deep=content (v1.3.0)

Light layer now stores only 1-sentence pointers to deep memories and
critical always-needed facts. Full content always goes to deep.

Hook now injects both light entries AND a topic index auto-derived from
deep entry tags, so Claude knows exactly what to memory_search without
loading everything upfront.

Skill updated with pointer pattern examples, decision flow, and red flags.
Tool descriptions updated to enforce 1-sentence limit on light entries.

commit.message:
DayView: redesign center column as live day timeline

Replace three disconnected cards (ProspectingKPIs, Appointments,
DailyFollowUps) with a single unified DayTimeline component:

Section 1 — KPI Pulse Bar (48px compact strip)
  6 metrics as icon + number only. Amber=zero, green=active.
  Hover tooltip shows metric label. + Appt button inline.

Section 2 — Live Day Timeline (7am–8pm, auto-scrolls to now)
  - Hour grid lines with muted labels (7am, 8am … 8pm)
  - 2px vertical timeline line on left edge
  - Appointment blocks: white card, 4px red left border,
    positioned at exact time on the grid. Click to edit.
    Checkbox to mark conducted. Green border+opacity when done.
  - Follow-up Action Queue: purple-tinted chip cluster anchored
    at 8am. Preserves all DailyFollowUps completion logic.
  - NOW indicator: brand-red dot + full-width horizontal rule +
    current time pill ("2:41 PM"). Updates every 60 seconds.
    "Up next: [client] at [time]" shown below the now line.
  - Auto-scrolls to center current time on mount (isToday only).
  - Empty state with CTA when no events or follow-ups.

Section 3 — EOD footer
  Shows "EOD Report due at 5:00 PM". Turns amber warning with
  countdown when within 2 hours. Red when past due.

WinsTodayCard preserved below the timeline.
All existing data fetching, interactions, and dark mode intact.

commit.message:
[compiler] Fix MXFP4 scale-read coalescing and 8-wave pingpong lowering (#1069)

…rnels

When M, N, K are dynamic, the read coalescer fails to merge per-thread
B-scale byte reads into contiguous vector<16xi8> loads due to
inconsistent floordiv/Mod evaluation at probe points that don't respect
divisibility constraints.

Changes:

- Apply divisibility forward subs (e.g. K -> 256*K') before numeric
probing so floordiv/Mod evaluate consistently across all probe sets.

- Replace symbolic diff approach with numeric probing for pairwise merge
to avoid symbolic explosion on complex preshuffle index expressions.
Uses parity-diverse probe generators (5 probes with three different
parity patterns) to catch Mod wrapping bugs that uniform-parity probes
miss.

- Allow re-merging reads with precomputed masks across merge levels.

- Pre-compute bounds checks as flat sympy booleans before merging.

- Support partial iter_arg groups (>=2 offsets) in opsel_scaled_mfma
coalescing, and look through arith.select from flatten-bounds masking.

- Fix extract_strided_slice in ASM backend to convert element offsets to
physical register indices based on element type width.

- Extract ProbeEvaluator class shared between merge functions, use
ceildiv, remove dead _resolve_symbolic_diff.

---------

Signed-off-by: Harsh Menon <harsh.menon@amd.com>
Co-authored-by: xintin <gaurav.verma@amd.com>
Co-authored-by: Hardcode84 <ivan.butygin@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: remove latency bottlenecks from action pipeline

- Remove client-side gas estimation from embedded wallet proxy (MUDContext.tsx)
  — the estimate was discarded by Thirdweb's wrappedCalls encoding (~300-400ms saved)
- Set MOVE_COOLDOWN = 0 in constants.sol — cooldown enforced client-side via
  isMovePending mutex + 200ms debounce instead of on-chain block.timestamp check
- Remove MoveTooFast retry logic (dead code with MOVE_COOLDOWN=0)
- Switch client RPC to self-hosted flashblocks-enabled node with Alchemy fallback
  (supportedChains.ts adds VITE_HTTPS_RPC_FALLBACK_URL to transport chain)

Server: enabled Base Flashblocks on reth node, added /public nginx path
(rate-limited, no auth, CORS) for browser clients.

Target: ~600-1200ms per action (down from 3-6s for consecutive moves).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix agent system: CEO cron generates briefs + test endpoint + Brevo email

Critical fix: Vercel crons make GET requests, but CEO brief generation
was only in POST handler. The cron was hitting GET which just returned
the last brief (empty) — never generating a new one.

Changes:
- CEO GET: if CRON_SECRET present, generate new brief (same as POST)
- CEO email: send to mrzirraro@gmail.com (founder), try Brevo first then Resend
- New /api/agents/test endpoint: admin-only, creates test prospect and
  sends all 3 cold email steps + triggers CEO brief for testing
- Extracted generateBrief() as shared function for GET cron and POST manual

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: LLM-driven next-keiko proposals after cooldown complete (#321)

* fix: auto-attribute katakaId from run.json in kime/maki record (#256)

When agents run with KATA_RUN_ID set, `kata kime record` and
`kata maki record` now automatically populate `katakaId` from
`run.json` when no explicit `--kataka` flag is given. The explicit
flag still overrides. `kansatsu record` already had this behaviour;
this brings kime and maki into parity.

- Add `katakaId` optional field to `DecisionEntrySchema`
- Add `katakaId` optional field to `ArtifactIndexEntrySchema`
- Add `--kataka <id>` option to `kata decision record`
- Add `--kataka <id>` option to `kata artifact record`
- Read `katakaId` from `readRun()` return value (no extra file read)
- 3 new tests for each command (auto-populate, override, absent)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: LLM-driven next-keiko proposals after cooldown complete (#313)

Introduces `NextKeikoProposalGenerator` — reads observations from cycle
run directories (friction/gap/insight), optionally fetches open GitHub
milestone issues via `gh issue list --json`, builds a synthesis prompt,
and invokes `claude --print` to produce 6-8 ranked bet proposals with
S/M/L appetite sizing and one-line signal rationale.

Key design decisions:
- Opt-in activation: generator only runs when `nextKeikoProposalGenerator`
  or `nextKeikoGeneratorDeps` is explicitly passed to `CooldownSession`
  (prevents existing tests from spawning claude during CI)
- Graceful degradation: milestone fetch failure and claude invocation
  failure both produce partial/fallback output rather than throwing
- Output appended to `formatCooldownSessionResult` after existing proposals
- CLI wired via `--milestone <name>` on `kata cooldown` and
  `kata cooldown complete` subcommands
- 14 new tests covering prompt generation, observation collection,
  milestone delegation, and all failure modes

Closes #313

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

feat: add checkpointing, conditional routing, deep graph queries, LangSmith tracing

1. LangGraph checkpointing: MemorySaver enables resumable pipeline flows,
   each invocation gets unique thread_id for state persistence

2. Conditional routing: pipeline routes error/fix through cross_reference
   for landmine detection; decision/pattern/feature skip to analyze

3. Deep SurrealDB graph queries: multi-hop traversals for decision impact
   analysis (decision->affects->file<-in_file<-error), error-fix chains,
   reverse edge lookups for file context

   on pipeline invocations, tagged LLM calls for filtering

5. README redesigned to showcase technical highlights for judges

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add tech debt tracking with TODO/FIXME scanner in CI (#1013)

## Summary

Adds technical debt tracking tooling to the repository, ensuring
TODO/FIXME comments are linked to GitHub issues and visible in build
output.

## Changes

1. **CI Workflow** (`.github/workflows/tech-debt-tracker.yml`): New
workflow that runs on push/PR to main, scanning for
TODO/FIXME/HACK/UNDONE markers and failing if any are not linked to a
GitHub issue (e.g., `TODO(#123)`).

2. **Scanner Script**
(`build/scripts/todo-scanner/Scan-TodoComments.ps1`): PowerShell script
that:
   - Scans source files for tech debt markers
- Validates each marker references a GitHub issue using `MARKER(#issue)`
format
   - Produces a summary table in GitHub Actions step summary
- Exits non-zero when unlinked markers are found (with
`-FailOnUnlinked`)

3. **S1135 Severity Upgrade** (`.editorconfig`): Changed SonarAnalyzer
rule S1135 (track TODO tags) from `suggestion` to `warning`, making TODO
comments visible as build warnings in SARIF output.

4. **Existing TODO Fix**: Linked the existing unlinked TODO in
`CallbackSignatureShouldMatchMockedMethodAnalyzer.cs` to the newly
created issue #1012.

## Why this improves the codebase

- Tech debt markers are now **enforced** to reference tracked issues,
preventing TODOs from being forgotten
- CI provides a clear report of all tech debt markers with their issue
links
- Build warnings surface TODOs through SonarAnalyzer S1135,
complementing the CI scanner

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Added an automated tech-debt scanner that finds TODO/FIXME/HACK/UNDONE
markers, reports unlinked items, and can fail CI when unlinked markers
are found.
* CI workflow runs the scanner on pushes and pull requests and publishes
a summarized report.

* **Chores**
  * Added a local pre-push hook to run the scanner before building.
  * Minor comment updated to reference an issue.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
relay: validate sha from GIT_VERSION before shell interpolation

botVersion() read the first token from GIT_VERSION with no validation,
then passed it as a git ref into execSync template strings in commitAge()
and gitRelation(). A poisoned GIT_VERSION file could inject shell commands.

Add /^[a-f0-9]{7,40}$/ guard to reject any non-SHA content before use.

commit.message:
ci: add release-drafter for automated release notes generation (#1015)

## Summary

Adds [Release
Drafter](https://github.com/release-drafter/release-drafter) to
automatically generate draft release notes from merged pull requests.

## Alignment with Project Release Process

This configuration was designed to align with the documented release
process in CONTRIBUTING.md and the style of existing releases (v0.4.0,
v0.4.1):

- **NBGV versioning respected**: The `version-resolver` is intentionally
omitted. Versioning is controlled by Nerdbank.GitVersioning via
`version.json`, not by release-drafter labels. The draft uses
`NEXT_PATCH_VERSION` as a placeholder; maintainers set the actual tag
from the NBGV-derived version when publishing.
- **Release branch workflow**: Header comments document that the draft
targets `main` but must be retargeted to the `release/vX.Y.Z` branch
before publishing, per the documented release branch strategy.
- **Template matches existing style**: The template includes
Contributors, Resources, and Feedback sections consistent with
v0.4.0/v0.4.1. An HTML comment reminds maintainers to polish the draft
with Highlights, Breaking Changes, etc. before publishing.
- **Autolabeler complements existing workflows**: Labels PRs based on
conventional commit prefixes (`feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `build:`,
`ci:`), complementing the existing `semantic-pr-check` and `labeler`
workflows.

## What this does

- **Workflow** (`.github/workflows/release-drafter.yml`): Runs on pushes
to `main` and on `pull_request_target` events to keep a draft release
updated.
- **Configuration** (`.github/release-drafter.yml`): Categorizes PRs
into sections (Analyzers, Features, Bug Fixes, Build & CI, Dependencies,
Documentation) based on existing labels.
- **Autolabeler**: Applies labels from conventional commit prefixes in
PR titles.

## Signal addressed

**Release Notes Automation** - Automated release notes or changelog
generation.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
* Added automated release draft generation on merges to main with
templated release names, tags, and organized changelog sections
(changes, contributors, resources, feedback).
* Introduced automatic pull request labeling based on title patterns to
categorize PRs (features, bugs, docs, build).
* Added CI workflows to update release drafts and apply labels
automatically.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Richard Murillo <rjmurillo@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: factory-droid[bot] <138933559+factory-droid[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

Remove project-specific context from blog-writer skill

Skill is intended for general use, not just Injective employees.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite video prompt architecture for correct duration + business-news link

Root cause: double Claude enhancement produced 400+ char scene prompts
that Seedance truncated, losing the --duration flag.

Architecture change:
- Remove redundant enhanceVideoPrompt step for video-long (was Claude call #1)
- Rewrite decomposePromptIntoScenes as SINGLE Claude call that:
  1. Reads the full creative brief (business + news context)
  2. Outputs SHORT purely visual scene prompts (max 200 chars each)
  3. No meta-instructions in output (no "ZERO text", no "CREATIVE BRIEF")
- startSeedanceT2V: clean prompt assembly
  - Strip meta-instructions, hard limit 200 chars visual content
  - Flags always first: --duration 10 --ratio 16:9 --camerafixed false
  - Total prompt to Seedance: ~250 chars (was 450+)
- Strong business-news link: Claude system prompt enforces visual connection
  in every scene (setting, props, character behavior reflect both themes)
- Fluid transitions: Claude enforces same camera direction, lighting,
  color palette across all segments

For 30s video: 3 segments x 10s, each with short focused visual prompt
